facts.text: Delaware Park first appeared as an unincorporated settlement in the mid‑1800s inside Lopatcong Township, which was formed in 1851 from parts of Greenwich and Harmony.
The township was originally called Phillipsburg Township until March 8 1861, when it was renamed Lopatcong after the nearby Lopatcong Creek.

By the 1800s, Delaware Park’s growth was spurred by the Morris Canal and railroads like the Central Railroad of New Jersey and the Lehigh Valley Railroad, which moved anthracite coal and boosted regional commerce.
Delaware Park is one of the original communities that formed the foundation of Lopatcong Township in Warren County, New Jersey.
